textPlain: Thank you so much for triggering this memory.I read a BASIC programming book when I was around 10, and the ELIZA example was hilarious and fascinating to me. I implemented several Eliza versions in secondary school as a way to learn new programming languages, and went on to study Computational Linguistics in university. Occasionally I  tried to find the book and particular Eliza example but failed, and I doubt I have the book now.When I saw the name Tim Hartnell, I knew it was him. I found the example on page 216 of Tim Hartnell's Giant Book of Computer Games.

I remember his books -- I had his one on adventure games. I always liked this sort of book by people like Hartnell and David Ahl that would have these long BASIC listings with lots of GOTOs and GOSUBs.
